title: storage — HTTP middleware
description: Five middleware. Every upload route composes at least the workspace scope + quota + MIME + size gates. The signed-URL redemption path composes an audit-hit middleware that verifies the signature + updates hit counters.

middleware.description: Sniffs the uploaded file's real MIME (finfo based) and validates against the target kind's allowed_mimes list. Rejects with HTTP 415 STORAGE_UNSUPPORTED_MIME on mismatch. Client-supplied Content-Type is IGNORED — only the byte sniff counts (client can lie).

Enforces the kind's max_size_mb ceiling. On single-request uploads, checks Content-Length header + actual byte count. On chunked uploads, checks Chunk-Size header against remaining allowance. Rejects with HTTP 413 STORAGE_FILE_TOO_LARGE.

Consumes the file's byte count against `storage.bytes.consumed` entitlement BEFORE the upload starts. On overquota rejects with HTTP 402 STORAGE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED + error envelope pointing at the plan upgrade URL. For chunked uploads, reserves the full total_size_bytes at initiate; on abort/expire, releases the reservation.

Applied to READ routes (download, signed-url issuance). Rejects with HTTP 423 STORAGE_FILE_NOT_AVAILABLE when the target file's virus_scan_state != 'clean'. Even the workspace admin cannot download an infected file; the platform-admin surface has its own quarantine-review flow with explicit consent + audit.

Stamps `workspace_id` onto every uploaded File from the caller's RequestContext AND rejects any read attempt whose file's workspace_id doesn't match the caller's active workspace. Belt-and-braces defence — the FilePolicy already gates individual actions, but this middleware short-circuits at the route level before hitting the controller.

Applied on the central-host redemption route ONLY (no auth). Hashes the signature portion of the URL, looks up SignedUrlAudit by signature_hash, verifies (not expired, not revoked, IP lock CIDR match if set, user lock match if set, one_time_use unconsumed). On success, increments hit_count via raw SQL (write-only hot path). On failure, returns HTTP 403 STORAGE_URL_INVALID.
